cards.back: A branch of digital logic that deals with the control of one or more signals over time, used for memory and timing functions.
Circuits that process information over time, using sequential logic to store and retrieve data.
Components that retain data even after the clock signal is removed, used in sequential logic circuits.
A type of storage element that can store a single bit of data, but requires an external clock signal to change the stored value.
A type of storage element that can store a single bit of data and can change the stored value on its own, without an external clock signal.
